(Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈɑfsneː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")) is a [sub-municipality](/wiki/Sub-municipality "Sub-municipality") of the [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") located in the [province of East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It was a separate [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") until 1977. On 1 January 1977, it was [merged](/wiki/Fusion_of_the_Belgian_municipalities "Fusion of the Belgian municipalities") into Ghent.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
["LISTE ALPHABETIQUE DES COMMUNES - Fusions de 1963 à 1977"](https://doc.statbel.fgov.be/publications/M000.03/M000.03FN_Comm_Gem_1963-1977.pdf) (PDF).

**Afsnee** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈɑfsneː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")) is a [sub-municipality](/wiki/Sub-municipality "Sub-municipality") of the [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") located in the [province of East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It was a separate [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") until 1977. On 1 January 1977, it was [merged](/wiki/Fusion_of_the_Belgian_municipalities "Fusion of the Belgian municipalities") into Ghent.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

It is situated at the banks of the river [Lys](/wiki/Lys_(river) "Lys (river)").

Maurice and [Anna De Weert](/wiki/Anna_De_Weert "Anna De Weert") bought a farm at Afsnee in 1895 beside the Lys which had belonged to a Dominican Monastery. Anna would paint there.[[2]](#cite_note-mystudio-2)
